titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Falconer Madan Collection of Oxford Manuscripts
titlestmt.author: Beinecke Staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0., has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 11891858.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday March 25, 2021 at 03:06
Addition of two boxes of material.

did.unittitle: Falconer Madan collection of Oxford manuscripts

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit. This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing, in 2014 and 2015.
The collection is comprised of material formerly classed as MS Vault 10:4 and MS Vault Shelves Oxford, Ip P725+C2, Lmd54 865+3, Ucu35 G6 +867i, Ovf86 O9+A9, Ovf86 O9+753P, Byzo 87+897n.

The finding aid for this collection is partially compiled from individual catalog cards that were created at or around the time of receipt by the library. Information from the catalog cards was repurposed in the finding aid in accordance with current archival descriptive standards. Some catalog cards, copies of which are filed with the collection, include more detailed descriptions of individual items and/or provenance notes. Descriptions for items not represented by catalog cards were derived from accession records, folder labels, or other available descriptive material.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

bioghist.head: Biographical/Historical note
bioghist.p: Falconer Madan (1851-1935) was the Librarian of the Bodleian Library at the University of Oxford, 1912-1919.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of a small group of Falconer Madan's papers relating to his research and writings, and original and copies of manuscripts relating to various individuals, and academic, social and historical events relating to Oxford. The majority of the material is bound in volumes, many of which contain the Yale University Library bookplate reading, "From the Collection of Oxford Books made by Falconer Madan, Bodley's Librarian." Original manuscripts include a group of twenty-three printed promises to support Sir Roundell Palmer in the Parliamentary election of 1868, signed by members of Oxford University, with related manuscript notes; a "Description of a Magdalen College, Oxford University gaudy" [student festival], "Phillipps MSS 9642"; and a scrapbook record of a marriage in London and a honeymoon in Oxford, with manuscript descriptions, and illustrated with engravings, press clippings, drawings, and printed ephemera by the groom Theodosius Purland. Additional material includes correspondence, verses, forms, various memoranda, and printed material relating to the city of Oxford and the University of Oxford, including the Oxford election of 1865, writings of Joseph Plowman, and the Iffley Lock.

did.unittitle: Falconer Madan papers
Copies of Beinecke Library catalog cards

Galley and page proofs of "A Bibliographical List of the Printed Works of Dr. Pusey," Appendix A in Life of Edward Bouverie Pusey by Henry Parry Liddon. With manuscript letters, notes and other material pertaining to the bibliography
Notes, newspaper clippings, and letters to Falconer Madan relating to the Oxford Movement and regarding The Tracts for the Times
"Notes of the Chief Papers Concerning Port Meadow in the Tin Box Labelled 'Port Meadow'," from the Oxford City Archives
"A Provisional List of the Papers Connected With the Oxfordshire Election of 1754"
Correspondence regarding Phi Beta Kappa Society and Oxford
Clippings of "Oxford Memories" articles by "Nestor" (William Tuckwell) from The Speaker ; with correspondence between Madan, Tuckwell, and others regarding the articles
Letters from Stephen Quelch to Falconer Madan bound with "Early Recollections of Oxford, by an old Freeman [Quelch]," reprinted from The Oxford Chronicle, May 6th and 13th, 1899

"The Great Charter of the University of Oxford (by Henry VIII), Translated into English," manuscript copy by Falconer Madan; with two other copies in Latin and correspondence with Madan relating to the making of the copy
Original manuscripts
Collection of twenty-three printed promises to support Sir Roundell Palmer in the Parliamentary election of 1868, signed by members of Oxford University; with related manuscript notes
Miscellaneous letters, notes, and clippings removed from the Madan copy of Oxford by Reverend Charles William Boase
Manuscript translation in an unidentified hand of parts of L'introduction à la vie devoté by Saint Francis de Sales
Manuscript verses possibly in the hand of [Daniel?] Harvey; with an autograph letter signed from Ruth Daniel to " Mr. Harvey" dated July 16, regarding the verses
Description of a Magdalen College, Oxford University gaudy [student festival], "Phillipps MSS 9642"
Articles on topography and biography of Oxfordshire, and other articles, extracts, orders and surveys
"The Famous Oxford Lozenges, prepared by W. P."
Manuscript directions to printer for "A Treatise on Logic, on the Basis of Aldrich" by Reverend John Huyshe; with printed pamphlet with manuscript corrections
Manuscript letters, printed broadsides, etc., relating to the poetry professorship contest of 1841-1842
Manuscript letters and printed matter relating to the Oxford Royal Regatta Jubilee celebration
Correspondence and printed material relating to Turrell's Hall, Oxford
Contracts, correspondence, receipts and other material relating to the presentation of Sailor's Knot and Pleasure at the New Theatre, Oxford
Signatures to elections at St. John's College
Correspondence, manuscript lists of guests, menu, printed material and other documents relating to the Mayor's dinner
Volume containing listings of place names
Notes, correspondence, printed notices and other documents relating to the movement coordinated by Frederick A. Dixey to preserve Greek at Oxford

Scrapbook record of a marriage in London and a honeymoon in Oxford, with manuscript descriptions, illustrated with engravings, press clippings, drawings, and printed ephemera by the bridal couple Theodosius and Mary Purland
Manuscript copies
"On the Dissolution of the Parliament at Oxford 1681"
"A Lay of Brazennose," manuscript copy, Oxford, 1866
"A Lay [of Merton College]," manuscript, 1865
"Reisebeschreibung" by Aulas Apronius [i.e. Adam Ebert],

"The Diary of a Vice-Chancellor, Sep 7 to Dec 4, 1697 (Dr. John Meare, Principal of Brasenose," manuscript copy in an unknown hand, edited by Falconer Madan for the Oxford Magazine; with the proof sheets of the article and correspondence relating to its publication
Manuscript concerning Carfax Conduit
Deed between the Burgesses of Oxford and Richard Owen of Godstowe, 1562
Letters and examination of witnesses in connection with the case of Mary Blandy, tried for poisoning her father, 1751
Rustica academiae Oxoniensis by John Allibond
"Facts and documents relative to the expulsion of six students, in 1768" from the papers of Henry Gough, and likely in his hand
"The Gentiad," a satirical poem by John Richard Green, 1857, with pencil annotations by [C?] Trevor
An account of printing at Oxford by John Bagford, 1714
Oxford Besiedged by John Taylor, 1645
Correspondence and other material relating to the city of Oxford and Oxford University
A-L
M-W
Scrapbooks relating to the city of Oxford and Oxford University
Writings by Joseph Plowman

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Note on flyleaf: "Everything in this volume was written by Mr. Joseph Plowman uncle of the mr. W. Plowman who sold it to me on Feb. 16, 1891 for 15/-. F. Madan"

Contents include: Joseph Plowman. Oxford idylls: by the city poet laureate, dedicated to his sponsors, Charles Neate ... and Charles James Sadler ... (Oxford: published for the author at Plowman's agency office, 1860). Printed copy.

did.unittitle: Oxford University Elections

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contents include Charles Lutwidge Dodgson, The dynamics of a particle (Oxford: J. Vincent, 1865). Printed copy.

did.unittitle: Iffley Lock and Thames Valley Drainage
Oxford City Officers and Elections 1660-1889, etc.
Oxford County Notices--Miscellaneous
New Municipal Buildings & Queen's Diamond Jubilee in Oxford
